heading: Student loan repayment program for Senate employees

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which the term of a departing Senator or Vice President ends; or

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which the departing Senator or Vice President will retire or resign.

children.heading: Departing Senator or Vice President
children.label: 2 Departing Senator or Vice President
children.text: The term “departing Senator or Vice President” means a Senator or Vice President who will not serve in the next term due to retirement, resignation, a decision to not seek reelection, or a failure to secure reelection.

children.label: A
children.text: who is an employee of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: whose rate of pay as an employee of the Senate, on the date on which such eligibility is determined, does not exceed the rate of basic pay payable for a position at level IV of the Executive Schedule under .section 5315 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: has the meaning given the term under ; andsection 1301 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: includes any employee of the Office of Congressional Accessibility Services whose pay is disbursed by the Secretary of the Senate.

children.label: A
children.text: means the employing office, as defined under , of an employee of the Senate; andsection 1301 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: includes the Office of Congressional Accessibility Services with respect to employees of that office whose pay is disbursed by the Secretary of the Senate.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Senate.

children.label: A
children.text: a loan made, insured, or guaranteed under part B, D, or E of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq., 1087a et seq., or 1087aa et seq.); and20 U.S.C. 1071

children.label: B
children.text: a health education assistance loan made or insured under part A of title VII of the Public Health Service Act ( et seq.), or under part E of title VIII of such Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 29242 U.S.C. 297a

children.heading: Senate student loan repayment program
children.label: b Senate student loan repayment program
i
children.text: the employing office shall agree to repay, by direct payments on behalf of the eligible employee, any student loan indebtedness of the eligible employee that is outstanding at the time the eligible employee and the employing office enter into the agreement, subject to this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the eligible employee shall agree to complete the required period of employment described in subsection (c)(1) with the employing office in exchange for the student loan payments.

children.label: I
children.text: the start and end dates of the required period of employment covered by the agreement;

children.label: II
children.text: the monthly amount of the student loan payments to be provided by the employing office;

children.label: III
children.text: the employee’s agreement to reimburse the Senate under the conditions set forth in subsection (d)(1);

children.label: IV
children.text: disclosure of the program limitations provided for in subsection (d)(4) and paragraphs (2), (3), (6), and (7) of subsection (f);

children.label: V
children.text: other terms to which the employing office and employee agree (such as terms relating to job responsibilities or job performance expectations); and

children.label: VI
children.text: any other terms prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Standard service agreements
children.label: ii Standard service agreements
children.text: The Secretary shall establish standard service agreements for employing offices to use in carrying out this section.

children.heading: Submission of agreements
children.label: 2 Submission of agreements
children.text: On entering into a service agreement under this section, the employing office shall submit a copy of the service agreement to the Secretary.

children.heading: Exclusion from participation in dual programs
children.label: 3 Exclusion from participation in dual programs
children.text: Notwithstanding , an employee of the Office of Congressional Accessibility Services may not participate in the student loan repayment program through an agreement under that section and participate in the student loan repayment program through a service agreement under this section at the same time.section 5379 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the term of the required period of employment under a service agreement under this section shall be 1 year. On completion of the required period of employment under such a service agreement, the eligible employee and the employing office may enter into additional service agreements for successive 1-year periods of employment.

children.heading: Departing Senators and Vice Presidents
children.label: B Departing Senators and Vice Presidents
i
children.text: is less than 1 year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall end on the last day of the last full pay period ending on or before the departure date of the departing Senator or Vice President.

children.label: i
children.text: $833 in any month; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a total of $80,000.

children.heading: Payments included in gross compensation limitations
children.label: B Payments included in gross compensation limitations
children.text: Any student loan payment made under this section in any month may not result in the sum of the payment and the compensation of an employee for that month exceeding ⁄th of the applicable annual maximum gross compensation limitation under section 4575(d)(2), (e), or (f) of this title.112

children.heading: Timing of payments
children.label: 3 Timing of payments
children.text: Student loan payments made under this section under a service agreement shall begin the first day of the pay period after the date on which the agreement is signed and received by the Secretary, and shall be made on a monthly basis.

children.heading: Loss of eligibility for student loan payments and obligation to reimburse
children.label: d Loss of eligibility for student loan payments and obligation to reimburse
A
children.text: the employee voluntarily separates from service with the employing office;

children.label: B
children.text: the employee engages in misconduct or does not maintain an acceptable level of performance, as determined by the head of the employing office; or

children.label: C
children.text: the employee violates any condition of the agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: funds are not made available to cover the cost of the student loan repayment program carried out under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: the employee and the head of the employing office involved mutually agree to terminate the service agreement;

children.label: C
children.text: the agreement is terminated as provided under subsection (f)(7)(A); or

children.label: D
children.text: the employee separates from service with the office of a departing Senator or Vice President.

children.heading: Another employing office
children.label: 3 Another employing office
children.text: An employing office who hires an eligible employee during a required period of employment (including a required period of employment described in subsection (c)(1)(B)) under such a service agreement may assume the remaining obligations (as of the date of the hiring) of the employee’s prior employing office under the agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: under or if the eligible employee is employed by any other office of the Senate or agency of the Federal Government; orsection 6568(c) of this titlesection 5514 of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: under other applicable provisions of law if the eligible employee is not employed by any other office of the Senate or agency of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Crediting of amounts
children.label: 5 Crediting of amounts
children.text: Any amount repaid by, or recovered from, an eligible employee under this section shall be credited to the subaccount for the employing office from which the amount involved was originally paid. Any amount so credited shall be merged with other sums in such subaccount for the employing office and shall be available for the same purposes, and subject to the same limitations (if any), as the sums with which such amount is merged.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of eligible employees that received student loan payments under this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of such payments made for each eligible employee;

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of any reimbursement amounts for early separation from service or whether any waivers were provided with respect to such reimbursements; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any other information determined to be relevant by the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate or the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate.

children.heading: Confidentiality
children.label: 2 Confidentiality
children.text: Such report shall not include any information which is considered confidential or could disclose the identity of individual employees or employing offices. Information required to be contained in the report of the Secretary under shall not be considered to be personal information for purposes of this paragraph.section 4108 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and maintain a central account from which student loan payments available under this section shall be paid on behalf of eligible employees.

children.heading: Office subaccounts
children.label: B Office subaccounts
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that, within the account established under subparagraph (A), a separate subaccount is established for each employing office to be used by each such office to make student loan payments under this section. Such student loan payments shall be made from any funds available to the employing office for student loan payments that are contained in the subaccount for the office.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: Amounts in each subaccount established under this paragraph shall not be made available for any purpose other than to make student loan payments under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the receipt by the Secretary of a signed service agreement; and

children.label: B
children.text: verification by the Secretary with the holder of the loan that the eligible employee has an outstanding student loan balance that qualifies for payment under this section.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: Student loan payments may be made under this section only with respect to the amount of student loan indebtedness of the eligible employee that is outstanding on the date on which the employee and the employing office enter into a service agreement under this section. Such payments may not be made under this section on a student loan that is in default or arrears.

children.heading: Payment on multiple loans
children.label: 4 Payment on multiple loans
children.text: Student loan payments may be made under this section with respect to more than 1 student loan of an eligible employee at the same time or separately, if the total payments on behalf of such employee do not exceed the limits under subsection (c)(2)(A).

children.heading: Treatment of payments
children.label: 5 Treatment of payments
children.text: Student loan payments made on behalf of an eligible employee under this section shall be in addition to any basic pay and other forms of compensation otherwise payable to the eligible employee, and shall be subject to withholding for income and employment tax obligations as provided for by law.

children.heading: No relief from liability
children.label: 6 No relief from liability
children.text: An agreement to make student loan payments under this section shall not exempt an eligible employee from the responsibility or liability of the employee with respect to the loan involved and the eligible employee shall continue to be responsible for making student loan payments on the portion of any loan that is not covered under the terms of the service agreement.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding the terms of a service agreement under this section, the head of an employing office may reduce the amount of student loan payments made under the agreement if adequate funds are not available to such office.

children.label: I
children.text: the employing office shall concurrently notify the eligible employee and the Secretary of the Senate of the reduction; and

children.label: II
children.text: not later than 30 days after the date of the concurrent notice, the eligible employee may terminate the service agreement.

children.label: i
children.text: the office has adequate funds available for the purpose of agreements under this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of the increased payment does not exceed the limitations under this section; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the total amount of the loan payments to be made (including such increase) during the remainder of the required period of employment does not exceed the amount of student loan indebtedness of the eligible employee as of the date of the increase.

children.heading: No right to continued employment
children.label: 8 No right to continued employment
children.text: A service agreement under this section shall not be construed to create a right to, promise of, or entitlement to the continued employment of the eligible employee.

children.heading: No entitlement
children.label: 9 No entitlement
children.text: A student loan payment under this section shall not be construed to be an entitlement for any eligible employee.

children.label: A
children.text: shall not be basic pay of an employee for purposes of chapters 83 and 84 of title 5 (relating to retirement) and chapter 87 of such title (relating to life insurance coverage); and

children.label: B
children.text: shall not be included in Federal wages for purposes of chapter 85 of such title (relating to unemployment compensation).

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of an employing office described in subsection (h)(1)(A), the amount described in that subsection for that fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an employing office described in subsection (h)(1)(B), the amount described in that subsection for that fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: the maximum amount for that employing office for that fiscal year; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the total amount is not sufficient to provide the maximum amount to each employing office, an amount that bears the same relationship to the total amount as the maximum amount for that employing office for that fiscal year bears to the total of the maximum amounts for all employing offices for that fiscal year.

children.heading: Apportionment
children.label: 3 Apportionment
children.text: In the case of an employing office that is a Committee of the Senate, the funds allocated under this subsection shall be apportioned between the majority and minority staff of the committee in the same manner as amounts are apportioned between the staffs for salaries.

children.label: A
children.text: For each employing office that is the personal office of a Senator, an amount equal to 2.5 percent of the total sums appropriated for the fiscal year involved for administrative and clerical salaries for such office.

children.label: B
children.text: For each other employing office, an amount equal to 2.5 percent of the total sums appropriated for the fiscal year involved for salaries for such office.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: Amounts provided under this section shall be subject to annual appropriations.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: i Effective date
children.text: This section shall apply to fiscal year 2002 and each fiscal year thereafter.
